heading: Great Lakes hypoxia and harmful algal blooms
label: 4005 Great Lakes hypoxia and harmful algal blooms
notes: CodificationSection was formerly set out in a note under , Conservation.section 1451 of Title 16
Prior ProvisionsA prior was renumbered section 607 and is classified to .section 606 of Pub. L. 105–383section 4006 of this title

Amendments2014— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section authorized appropriations for research, education, and monitoring activities related to the prevention, reduction, and control of harmful algal blooms and hypoxia for fiscal years 1999 to 2010.Pub. children.text: Federal agencies;
State agencies;
regional research consortia;
academia;
private industry; and
nongovernmental organizations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Task Force shall develop and submit to the Congress a plan, based on the integrated assessment under subsection (a), for reducing, mitigating, and controlling hypoxia and harmful algal blooms in the Great Lakes.June 30, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: address the monitoring needs identified in the integrated assessment under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: develop a timeline and budgetary requirements for deployment of future assets;

children.label: i
children.text: all assumptions built into the models; and

children.label: ii
children.text: data quality methods used to ensure the best available data are utilized; and

children.label: i
children.text: characterizing current and past biological conditions in ecosystems affected by hypoxia and harmful algal blooms; and

children.label: ii
children.text: quantifying effects, including economic effects, at the population and community levels.

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate with State and local governments;

children.label: B
children.text: consult with representatives from academic, agricultural, industry, and other stakeholder groups, including relevant Canadian agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: ensure that the plan complements and does not duplicate activities conducted by other Federal or State agencies;

children.label: D
children.text: identify critical research for reducing, mitigating, and controlling hypoxia events and their effects;

children.label: E
children.text: evaluate cost-effective, incentive-based partnership approaches;

children.label: F
children.text: ensure that the plan is technically sound and cost effective;

children.label: G
children.text: utilize existing research, assessments, reports, and program activities;

children.label: H
children.text: publish a summary of the proposed plan in the Federal Register at least 180 days prior to submitting the completed plan to Congress; and

children.label: I
children.text: after submitting the completed plan to Congress, provide biennial progress reports on the activities toward achieving the objectives of the plan.
